ssml_content: <speak><prosody rate="fast">Washing clothes is science, drying them is an art, but folding them is a relentless, unending <emphasis level="strong">punishment</emphasis> from the universe.</prosody> The clean clothes sit in a pile on that one specific chair in your bedroom. The laundry chair. Days pass. The pile watches you sleep. It judges your procrastination. <break time="1s"/> You start living out of the basket, digging through the fabric mountain every morning like an urban archaeologist. <break time="2s"/> <prosody rate="slow">Are we defined by the clothes we wear, or by the clothes we refuse to put in the closet? Confess your laundry chair sins below.</prosody></speak>
